plan_section: Step 1: Add overlay sound asset plumbing, branding corrections, and non-visual shell integration seams

plan_alignment.notes: The staged implementation matches Step 1: sound bootstrap asset is added and injected after the shell script, optional /sound recursive copying is present, nginx routes denvermc-sound and /sound/, and map-shell branding now uses /brand/linux/256x256.png with home-link behavior.

correctness_safety.notes: No audio playback or Web Audio initialization is introduced. The new nginx routes remain narrow to the named overlay asset and /sound/ prefix, and the shell logo path resolves through the existing Next public asset surface.

code_quality.notes: Changes are small, readable, and consistent with the existing overlay asset injection and singleton browser-script patterns.

completeness.notes: Acceptance criteria are satisfied for asset copy/injection, static routing, bootstrap globals, map logo path/link behavior, and favicon asset correction. Reported lint, typecheck, overlay apply, diff check, and CoreScope cleanliness checks are adequate for this step.

patterns.notes: Implementation stays in overlay-owned files and nginx routing, avoids direct vendor/CoreScope edits, and preserves the existing Next metadata icons contract.

integration.notes: The overlay script order works with CoreScope body script loading, /brand assets continue to be served by Next, and the /sound/ route does not shadow unrelated Next routes.

flags.note: The root favicon.ico now contains PNG image data rather than an ICO container. Browsers commonly tolerate this, and metadata points at /brand/win/colorado-mesh.ico, but use a true ICO container if strict favicon.ico consumers matter.
Staged deletions of prior Forge review/step artifacts are outside the runtime Step 1 asset-plumbing scope; acceptable if this is intentional Forge reset cleanup.
